response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a strong software craftsmanship background, but he is critically mismatched for this role. The most disqualifying factor is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, his technical stack is predominantly web-oriented (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js) with no evidence of embedded networking, L2/L3 security protocols, Linux kernel modification experience, or any of the required security technologies such as MACsec or 802.1X. His C/C++ experience is limited to a single role at National Instruments over a decade ago.
strengths: 15+ years of varied software development experience across web, mobile, and some embedded contexts
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments, demonstrating foundational low-level programming background
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency mentioned anywhere — this is a stated hard requirement and an immediate disqualifier
Technical stack is almost entirely web/cloud-focused (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB); no embedded networking, switch firmware, or L2/L3 security protocol experience
No evidence of Linux kernel development, kernel vulnerability work, or chip-vendor SDK familiarity required for this embedded security role

key_factors.factor: Long software career with some C exposure
